signal: Anthropic's double opsec failure in five days is the asymmetric signal of the week. On 26 March a CMS misconfiguration revealed Claude Mythos — a new model tier above Opus with Anthropic's own draft d

delta_strip.title: Anthropic dual opsec failure: KAIROS/BUDDY/undercover mode + Mythos leak
delta_strip.one_line: Two unforced disclosures in five days expose systemic internal controls failure at the lab with the largest DoD-adjacent AI footprint — including an ‘undercover mode’ that suppresses codenames from git commit histories.

delta_strip.title: GSA ‘any lawful use’ clause: comment deadline April 3 — generalises Pentagon Anthropic playbook to all federal AI
delta_strip.one_line: GSA clause would require irrevocable ‘any lawful’ use rights and ban non-US AI from all federal contracts — codifying no-safety-restrictions as standard federal procurement, with False Claims Act liability up the AI supply chain.

delta_strip.title: OpenAI $852B valuation at 60x revenue — most extreme in tech history
delta_strip.one_line: Round closes with retail investor tranche, converting OpenAI from venture-funded startup to quasi-sovereign infrastructure entity — requiring aggressive monetisation that will reshape API pricing, government contracting, and competitive dynamics.

delta_strip.title: AISI pipeline: all three principal AI safety bodies in leadership transition approaching August 2026 enforcement cliff
delta_strip.one_line: UK AISI (interim director since October 2025), US CAISI (restructured, capacity-reduced), EU AI Office (no new senior appointments) — simultaneous leadership vacuum at the most consequential governance moment since the Paris AI Action Summit.

delta_strip.title: EU AI Act Omnibus: 28 April trilogue agreement target — Standards Vacuum 122 days to deadline
delta_strip.one_line: The 28 April target is the entire risk management window for Europe’s AI ecosystem — a single failed trilogue session creates the August 2026 compliance cliff; Standards Vacuum is arithmetically impossible to close regardless.

signal: OpenAI's cancellation of the $1B Disney Sora deal and reallocation of video-synthesis compute to robotics training is the structural event of the week — not a product pivot but a capability reallocation signal. Labs that recognise embodied AI pre-training as the next compute frontier now have a 12-month lead over those still benchmarking on language. Meanwhile, China formalized "ciyuan" (词元) as the official Mandarin term for AI token at the highest state level, explicitly framing it as a settlement unit for the intelligent era. Geopolitical AI architecture is no longer being built only in data centers — it is being built in terminology committees and standards bodies. Watch both vectors.

delta_strip.title: Ciyuan: China formalises AI tokens as settlement currency
delta_strip.one_line: China's National Data Administration officially designates the AI 'token' as an economic settlement unit, framing it as a value anchor for the intelligent era — the first move in a potential token-denominated AI trade framework targeting Global South markets.

delta_strip.title: OpenAI kills Sora/Disney — redirects compute to robotics pre-training
delta_strip.one_line: Not a content pivot. A signal that video-synthesis compute is being reallocated to embodied AI pre-training — labs that read this correctly now have a 12-month robotics capability lead.

delta_strip.title: Langflow CVE-2026-33017 exploited within 20 hours — AI orchestration stack now established attack surface
delta_strip.one_line: CISA KEV addition confirms agentic AI orchestration infrastructure (Langflow, LiteLLM, n8n) is the new enterprise attack surface — a single compromised orchestration layer gives lateral access across the entire connected data estate.

delta_strip.title: Ecolab $4.75B CoolIT acquisition — water company becomes AI infrastructure gatekeeper
delta_strip.one_line: A $74B water treatment company paying 17x the prior investment price for a 5% market penetration cooling business is the clearest signal yet that physical thermal management is the next AI infrastructure moat.

delta_strip.title: The AI Scientist — first AI-authored paper passes peer review; Nature calls for governance response
delta_strip.one_line: Quality scales with model capability — this system becomes dramatically more capable as frontier models improve. Nature's call for institutional response signals the scientific establishment recognises a phase transition that governance is weeks to months behind.
